chief_complaint: A 53-year-old male with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease and rectal prolapse underwent the Altemeier procedure, presenting with sepsis and anastomotic leakage.
history: The patient had a history of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease and rectal prolapse. He underwent the Altemeier procedure. On postoperative day 4, he developed sepsis secondary to anastomotic leakage.
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination revealed signs of sepsis, anastomotic leakage, presacral infection, and acute exacerbation of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
labs: Procalcitonin and C-reactive protein levels were elevated. Other investigations: not provided.
differentials: Acute exacerbation of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
correct_ranking: Acute exacerbation of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease
